data.title: Apple's Much-Needed Selloff Is Here - Resilient Prospects Trigger Buy Rating
data.description:
I previously covered Apple Inc. (AAPL) in January 2026, discussing why I had reiterated my Hold rating then, attributed to the minimal margin of safety arising from the expensive valuations through the previously baked-in iPhone 17 replacement cycle in China and the priced-in iPhone Fold exuberance.

This was worsened by the deteriorating balance sheet health along with the potential double-digit correction from prior inflated levels if the stock retraced as per the three historical trading cycles since early 2021.

In this article, I shall discuss why I am upgrading the AAPL stock as a Buy nearer to my Buy Zones of $230s, thanks to the ongoing momentum reversal from the memory supply crunch and the market rotation.

My optimism is attributed to their ability to command the premium iPhone pricing at a time of "super upgrade cycle," the increasingly sticky iOS ecosystem/the consistently growing Services segment, and the healthier balance sheet on a YoY basis.

AAPL's Prospects Likely To Remain Resilient

For now, AAPL has continued to trade sideways along the September 2025 trading floor, with it lending credibility to my last two Hold ratings, with a similar underperformance also observed in the wider market and Big Tech peers in varying degrees.

Part of their headwinds may be attributed to the ongoing market rotation worsened by its likely to be impacted gross margins from the ongoing memory/storage supply crunch, with AAPL likely to face a tougher YoY comparison on their gross margins in the near term.

1. Focus on Premium Models

Otherwise, based on AAPL's FQ2 '26 guidance across:

  • revenue growth by +14.5% YoY at the midpoint,
  • service revenues at approximately +14% YoY, and
  • gross margins of 48.5% at the midpoint (+0.4 points QoQ/+1.5 YoY/+10.7 from FY2019 levels of 37.8%),

It appears that AAPL's profitable growth prospects may remain rather resilient, pending further clarity in the upcoming earnings call scheduled for April 30, 2026 (post-market).

This is especially since the iPhone 17 launch and the consequently robust consumer demand have triggered AAPL's expanding global smartphone share at 25% in Q4 '25 (+7 points QoQ/+2 points YoY), at the expense of the first and second runners-up.

This development lends strength to the Counterpoint analysts' conjecture surrounding the "super upgrade cycle" with FQ2 '26 likely to bring forth another quarter of robust top-line performance, as their installed base supposedly reaches all-time highs for both upgraders/switches through the successful iPhone 17 launch in China.

This is a material development indeed, since the robust top-line performance may temper part of the higher COGS arising from the ongoing memory supply crunch.

At the same time, AAPL has hinted at a "strong price cycle" for iPhone 17, with it implying their ongoing price hikes and focus on premium mix to drive stable profit margins - all of which lends credibility to Counterpoint expecting premium handset market leaders, including AAPL, to remain resilient during the challenging supply environment.

If anything, I am of the opinion that the ongoing headwind has been a boon for the Cupertino giant, since it may allow further market share gains in the premium segment as some handset OEMs already "take actions to reduce their handset build plans and channel inventory."

For now, I urge readers to closely monitor AAPL's commentary in the upcoming earnings call, since their stock price recovery hinges on their ability to command a premium pricing on their iPhones/smart devices during the likely to be prolonged memory supply crunch through H2'27, before the new production capacity kicks in.

2. Services/iOS Moat

Beyond the hardware pessimism, readers may want to note AAPL's true moat in the Services segment, which continues to grow in revenues to $30.13B in FQ1'26 (+14.3% YoY) and gross margins to 76.5% (+1.5 points YoY/+12.8 from FY2019 levels of 63.7%).

Similar to Nvidia's (NVDA) CUDA ecosystem, AAPL's strategy across the iPhones, Macs, iPads, and Wearables/Home/Accessories platforms encourages consumers to stay within the iOS ecosystem and buy more Apple products - all of which are already driving their higher global installed base.

These developments directly contributed to AAPL's "higher net sales from advertising, the App Store, and cloud services" in the Services segment, with it underscoring the company's excellent hardware and ecosystem monetization trends.

This is especially since their App Store revenues of $52.5B in 2025 (+0.6% YoY) very well exceed their direct peers, Google Play (GOOG) at $30B (+2.8% YoY) and Steam at $11.7B (+13% YoY), with the iOS customers' elevated in-app spending share of 55.7% against the "29.25% market share in the global smartphone industry" being extremely impressive.

This is on top of the market analysts already expecting the global in-app purchase market size to continue expanding from $195.74B in 2024 to $1.28T by 2034, expanding at an accelerated CAGR of +20.7%, with it implying the App Store's multi-year growth prospects.

Combined with the Services segment comprising a larger portion of its overall top lines at a ratio of 26.2% in FY2025 (+1.7 points YoY/+8.5 from FY2019 levels of 17.7%), I am of the opinion that AAPL's Services segment is likely to be a top/bottom-line driver in the long term, despite the near-term hardware/memory headwinds.

3. Sideways Trading Triggering Valuation Moderation

The same quiet optimism has also been embedded in the consensus forward estimates, with AAPL expected to report robust top/bottom-line growth at a CAGR of +8.3%/+11.6% through FY2028, not too far from its 10Y CAGR of +5.9%/+12.5%, despite the deceleration from 5Y CAGR of +8.7%/+17.9%, respectively.

Combined with the -13.7% correction from the 52-week highs and the sideways trading since September 2025, it is unsurprising that AAPL has finally grown into its previously premium valuations at the current FWD P/E non-GAAP valuations of 29.25x, nearer to the 5-year mean of 28.69x and down from the recent highs of 34.80x.

This development has already contributed to the notably cheaper, estimated 3Y PEG non-GAAP ratio of 2.52x, based on the FWD P/E non-GAAP valuations of 29.25x and the consensus adj EPS growth estimates at a 3Y CAGR of +11.6%, down from the recent highs of 3x and the 5Y mean of 2.68x.

On the other hand, it goes without saying that AAPL is still trading at a notable premium compared to its peers, including:

  • Smartphone/DRAM and NAND - Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. (SSNLF) at FWD P/E non-GAAP valuation of 7.19x and 3Y PEG non-GAAP ratio of 0.11x, and
  • smartphone/automaker - Xiaomi Corporation (XIACY) at 23.96x/1.98x, respectively,

with a similar conclusion also observed when compared to its Magnificent 7 peer group P/E median of 23.69x and 3Y PEG non-GAAP ratio median of 1.65x, including:

  • advertising - Meta (META) at 17.39x/1.65x,
  • general AI accelerator - NVDA at 20.20x/0.49x,
  • hyperscaler/enterprise AI - Microsoft (MSFT) at 21.33x/1.19x,
  • hyperscaler/advertising - GOOG at 23.69x/1.85x,
  • hyperscaler/commerce/advertising - Amazon (AMZN) at 25.73x/1.43x, and
  • automaker - Tesla (TSLA) at 173.93x/4.95x, respectively.

This is especially since AAPL's balance sheet health appears mixed at a net debt position of $21.6B in FQ1'26 (+47.3% YoY/-188% from FY2019 net debt of $7.49B).

It is apparent from these developments that their shareholder returns at share repurchases of $90.71B in FY2025 (-4.4% YoY) and dividend payouts of $15.42B in FY2025 (+1.2% YoY) have occurred faster than their Free Cash Flow generation of $98.77B in FY2025 (-9.2% YoY), a trend that has similarly occurred in FY2024, FY2021, FY2020, and FY2019.

As a result of the still expensive valuations and the mixed balance sheet health, I am of the opinion that it may be more prudent to wait for a further stock price correction for an improved margin of safety.

AAPL Is Upgraded To A Buy Near $230s

For now, AAPL has continued to trade within the established uptrend pattern over the past few years, with the ongoing pullback since the 2025 top implying a further correction to the base uptrend support line/Q3'24 resistance levels of $230s.

This conjecture is not overly bearish as well, with AAPL now entering oversold territories and a further correction to the historical support likely, given the increasingly pessimistic stock market sentiments before the market enters the Q1'26 earnings season in April/May 2026.

This is especially since the base uptrend support line/Q3'24 resistance levels of $230s is nearer to my fair value estimates of $226.90, based on the 5Y P/E mean of 28.69x and the LTM adj EPS performance of $7.91 (+13.4% sequentially).

A further correction to those levels may also trigger an expanded upside potential of +29% to my long-term price target of $297.20, based on the consensus FY2028 adj EPS estimates of $10.36, with it lending credence to my reiterated patience before my Buy Zones materialize.

As a result of the likely to improve risk/reward from the ongoing momentum reversal, their ability to command the premium iPhone pricing at a time of "super upgrade cycle," and the increasingly sticky iOS ecosystem/the consistently growing Services segment, I am reiterating my Buy rating for the AAPL stock nearer to my Buy Zones of $230s.

Given the potential -7.2% downside from current levels, I urge some patience in the meantime.

data.title: Tesla: The Market Will Eventually Ignore The Carefully Crafted Disruption Narrative
data.description:
Tesla, Inc.'s (TSLA) market cap is built on intense investor hype over its suite of pending launches that hold promise for market disruption and large respective total addressable markets ("TAM"), but with the Texan automaker's valuation entirely unhinged from the reality of its financials. TSLA's valuation is the elephant in the room, reflective of a business model built around a carefully crafted narrative of future growth and creative disruption that has baked in a lot of promise into its current stock price. I've described this as a Potemkin village, an unstable facade created to hide a harsher reality behind it. For TSLA, this is a reality of declining sales, waning profitability, and a historically elevated valuation multiple that has become unanchored from fundamentals.

I am short, with my thesis hinged on 2026 bringing back-to-back quarters that show TSLA's poorer financials forcing the market to confront the reality behind the village. This village will not only see its Optimus Robot outperforming human surgeons in the operating room by the end of 2028, but it will also include the Robotaxis being "widespread" across the U.S. by the end of this year.

This village hinges on TSLA's eventual provision of an autonomous ride-hailing service across the U.S. and its core Western European markets. For bulls, the Robotaxi wouldn't just disrupt the current $82.65 billion market for taxi services and ride-hailing in the U.S. It could help reshape and expand this market and play a key role in a market forecasted to grow by a 7.6% compound annual growth rate to $159.13 billion in 2033. However, even though Robotaxi services have really only essentially been made available for trials in Austin and San Francisco, the market has ascribed to TSLA a valuation based on a significant multiple that's simply not been extended to any other company. TSLA's previous convergence with the Magnificent 7, as per its price to trailing twelve months earnings, has exploded to a level of absurdity.

The company is swapping hands for a 336x P/E ratio, almost 10x Nvidia Corporation's (NVDA) multiple. Hence, profits simply don't matter when it comes to TSLA, but the bearish thesis rests on this current dynamic being transient through the lens of time. To be clear, TSLA is experiencing weaker demand in its core markets but trades on a multiple far in excess of the most profitable firms in the history of U.S. capitalism, including a company that's a global leader in the AI revolution.

I last covered TSLA with a Strong Sell as my 2026 short on the back of a pending dip in sales following a third-quarter surge as car buyers in the U.S. rushed to meet the deadline for EV tax credit. The stock is down 21% since then, and has far more room to fall.

Revenue, Profits, And Cash Flow Deteriorate

TSLA generated fourth quarter revenue of $24.9 billion, down 3.1% over its year-ago comp and a beat by $140 million on consensus estimates. This was on the back of 418,227 deliveries, down 15.87% sequentially from the third quarter and by 77,343 vehicles from its year-ago comp. The impact of this broad weakness in deliveries cannot be overstated. TSLA is losing revenue at a time when EV sales are reaching maturity in some of its core markets. Deliveries have plateaued since 2023, but the stock is up at least 88% since then.

This divergence between its stock price and revenue becomes more baffling once the dip in profitability and cash flow comes into view. Net income on a GAAP basis came in at $840 million, down around 61% from its year-ago comp. Further, free cash flow dipped 30% year-over-year to $1.42 billion. For some context, NVDA recorded free cash flow of $34.9 billion for its last reported quarter. This was up more than 100% from its year-ago comp.

It's important to consider the core bullish thesis and risk for short positions that TSLA does not trade in line with its peers or even the Magnificent 7 because it has such a broadly diversified business model with a ton of downstream growth opportunities. Autonomous ride-hailing is set to be a huge global market, and a revolutionary humanoid robot would possibly unlock a multi-trillion dollar TAM. This would be from a material chunk of human labor, from surgeons to factory workers, being able to be automated.

Conclusion

TSLA's fundamentals will continue to take a dip this year, with revenue set for another year-over-year decline, net income to further worsen, and free cash flow to move further to the downside on the erosion of the highly lucrative regulatory tax credits sales. In my opinion, 2026 will likely not see Robotaxis become "widespread" across the U.S., apart from continued limited trials in specific metro areas. I am again rating TSLA stock as a Strong Sell.
